description: Analyze single-family housing underutilization across San Francisco zones using UrbanSim microdata in the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/sfh_underutilization.ipynb. Using the 'households', 'buildings', and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, identify "Occupied Large Single-Family Homes (SFH)" defined as buildings with one residential unit, at least 2000 square feet, and at least one person. Classify these homes as "Underutilized" if they have two or fewer persons. For each zone (zone_id), calculate the total number of large SFHs, the number of underutilized SFHs, and the underutilization rate. Filter for zones with at least 20 total large SFHs and identify the top 20 zones by underutilization rate. Export these top 20 zones to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/underutilized_zones.csv with the columns: zone_id, total_large_sfh, underutilized_sfh, and underutilization_rate. Save a bar chart of the underutilization rates for these zones to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/underutilization_plot.png and ensure all notebook cells are executed.
